topic: Review of 004-deferred-followups — implementation code for 10 Gate 7 deferred items

findingDetails.title: Dual-path folder-existence check is redundant but not harmful
findingDetails.evidence: Shell checks folder existence before delegating; orchestrator.ts:349-351 has identical check. Both paths agree on exit 3 per ADR-003.
findingDetails.recommendation: Accept as defense-in-depth. No change needed.
findingDetails.scopeProof: validate.sh shell path + orchestrator.ts Node path converge on same exit code

findingDetails.title: findSkillRoot fallback uses hardcoded relative path
findingDetails.evidence: After 8-level walk fails, falls back to path.resolve(startDir, ../../..) which may be wrong. resolver has its own fallback but TEMPLATE_ROOT depends on this.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use same fallback as resolver or throw instead of returning likely-wrong path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: findSkillRoot return value feeds TEMPLATE_ROOT used by renderedTemplate()

findingDetails.title: Version mismatch between shell script and orchestrator
findingDetails.evidence: Shell declares VERSION=2.0.0 at line 29; orchestrator prints v3.0.0 at line 426. Both display in user-facing output.
findingDetails.recommendation: Sync shell VERSION to match orchestrator or remove from shell.
findingDetails.scopeProof: validate.sh VERSION variable vs orchestrator printReport version string

findingDetails.title: Dead level parameter in loadManifest()
findingDetails.evidence: level parameter only used in error throw at line 98; cache-hit path at line 89 never touches it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove level parameter or rename to signal error-context-only role.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All callers pass level but function ignores it for cache hits

findingDetails.title: Fence regex divergence between stripFences and renderInlineGates
findingDetails.evidence: stripFences uses literal backticks; renderInlineGates uses \x60 hex. Behaviorally identical but maintenance hazard.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Both functions in same module, duplicated logic

findingDetails.title: collectKnownSessionIds scans all .md files without bounds
findingDetails.evidence: Recursive walk of entire .opencode/specs/ tree with synchronous reads. No cache or bailout.
findingDetails.recommendation: Cache known IDs or add file-count bailout.

findingDetails.title: ADR-001 special case in validateTemplateShape fragile
findingDetails.evidence: Special-case regex ^ADR-001: assumes single ADR-001 header at cursor. Works for current structure but fragile.
findingDetails.recommendation: Document or replace with dedicated template placeholder.

findingDetails.title: copy_templates_batch silently returns 0 when no templates
findingDetails.evidence: When template_paths array is empty, function returns 0 with no output. Callers may assume success with files created.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add warning or validate post-batch that at least one file was created.
findingDetails.scopeProof: create.sh uses contract docs from resolve_level_contract; empty list would be a contract error

findingDetails.title: --out-dir uses filePath.split on Windows-unsafe path
findingDetails.evidence: filePath.split('/').pop() assumes Unix paths. Node path.basename() is platform-safe.

findingDetails.title: SECTIONS_PRESENT always passes without checking
findingDetails.evidence: entry('SECTIONS_PRESENT', 'pass', ...) is always-pass; anchor checking is the real enforcement. Misleading rule name.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename to SECTIONS_DELEGATED or remove the entry.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Anchor validation subsumes section checking; name is misleading

findingDetails.title: Bash and Node level detection have divergent patterns
findingDetails.evidence: Shell has 2 additional detection patterns (bullet metadata, anchored inline) that Node lacks. Docs using these formats would be misdetected by Node orchestrator.
findingDetails.recommendation: Sync detection patterns between shell and Node detectLevel().
findingDetails.scopeProof: Shell detect_level vs orchestrator detectLevel both run for the same folder

findingDetails.title: validate.sh has template hash validation that orchestrator lacks
findingDetails.evidence: validate_template_hashes() checks MD5 hashes (informational only). Orchestrator has no equivalent. Gap if hash validation ever becomes blocking.
findingDetails.recommendation: Document gap or remove legacy hash validation.
